dataset: SRTM (Shuttle Radar Topography Mission) Elevation Data

pi_items.verified_value: /sdcard/com.ds.avare (valid Avare storage location; /sdcard aliases to /storage/emulated/0 on modern Android)
pi_items.source: Avare forum posts and Android storage documentation

pi_items.verified_value: /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.ds.avare (primary external storage location for Avare app data)
pi_items.source: Avare user forums and Android app-specific storage documentation

pi_items.verified_value: /sdcard/avare (fallback directory mentioned in setup scripts as alternative Avare storage)
terrain (reasonable file name pattern for terrain/elevation data files)
pi_items.source: Standard naming convention for elevation data; search results confirm Avare can display 'Terrain/Elevation Maps'

pi_items.verified_value: elev (reasonable file name pattern for elevation data files)
pi_items.source: Standard naming convention for elevation data; Avare documentation mentions elevation maps
No specific verification found for '.t' file extension in Avare context
No specific verification found for '.dt1' file extension in Avare context. .dt1 is used in LandSerf 2 for terrain data but not confirmed for Avare.

pi_items.verified_value: .hgt (NASA SRTM Shuttle Radar Topography Mission file format)
pi_items.source: SRTM data uses .hgt extension for elevation files. 1°x1° tiles with 1201x1201 (3-arc-second) or 3601x3601 (1-arc-second) grids. Confirmed as standard elevation data format used by aviation applications.
SRTM elevation data actual file sizes: 3-arc-second HGT (~3 MB), 1-arc-second HGT (~25 MB), BIL format minimum (~1 MB). All verified sources show elevation data files are minimum 1 MB, not 10 KB.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves downloading real SRTM (Shuttle Radar Topography Mission) elevation data from USGS sources for the San Francisco/Northern California region in the Avare aviation GPS app. The three data directory locations are verified as legitimate Android storage paths used by Avare. File patterns for .hgt (SRTM standard format), 'terrain', and 'elev' are verified. However, the minimum file size claim of 10,240 bytes is contradicted by actual SRTM data: standard 3-arc-second HGT files are approximately 3 MB, 1-arc-second HGT files are approximately 25 MB, and compressed BIL format files are minimum 1 MB. The 10 KB minimum size is orders of magnitude smaller than any real elevation dataset.
